text.title: Testing with pytest Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of testing-pytest enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Use function-style tests with fixture injection. Convert `unittest.TestCase` classes to functions unless the codebase enforces class style.
rule.rationale: Function tests get fixture injection, parametrize, and plugin hooks. Class style limits all three.

rule.statement: Choose fixture scope by lifetime: `function` (default), `class`, `module`, `session`. Session scope only for read-only setup (test DB connection).
rule.rationale: Wrong scope is the #1 cause of cross-test pollution. Session-scope mutable state poisons unrelated tests.

rule.statement: Use `pytest.mark.parametrize` for ≥3 cases sharing the same logic. For ≥6 cases, use `ids=` so failures name themselves.
rule.rationale: Parametrize gives one test name per case; manual loops conceal which case failed.

rule.statement: Mock external collaborators with `mocker.patch` from `pytest-mock`, not `monkeypatch.setattr` for stdlib/3rd-party modules.
rule.rationale: `mocker.patch` auto-cleans on teardown; `monkeypatch` is fine for env vars but verbose for module attrs.

rule.statement: Async tests use `pytest-asyncio` with `@pytest.mark.asyncio` and `asyncio_mode = strict` in config.
rule.rationale: Auto mode hides forgotten `await`; strict mode forces every async test to opt in explicitly.

rule.statement: Configure coverage in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.coverage.*]`; fail CI under threshold (suggested 80% for new code).
rule.rationale: Inline `--cov-fail-under` works but TOML keeps the policy versioned with the code.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
